prose:
Fukui reactivity indices for aniline
• Property: condensed Fukui indices (f⁺, f⁻) identifying the most electrophilic and most nucleophilic sites
• Level of theory: DFT, tier `standard` (B3LYP/def2-TZVP, density-fitted RIJK)
• Geometry: single-point on user-supplied `/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/aniline/aniline.xyz` (no prior optimization)
• Solvent: gas phase (implicit only)
• Charge / multiplicity: 0 / 1 (neutral singlet)
• Software: PySCF via chemkit CLI (invocation: `/home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py fukui --method dft --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --solvent gas-phase --charge 0 --multiplicity 1 --tier standard /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/aniline/aniline.xyz --out /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmpsxnodqrk/live.json`)
• Convergence: SCF converged for all states; see warnings
• Most electrophilic site: C atom 3 (1-based indexing), f⁺ = 2.2975867
• Most nucleophilic site: N atom 1 (1-based indexing), f⁻ = 0.20452359
• Result JSON: `/home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmpsxnodqrk/live.json`
• Live log: streaming to `fukui_20260703-191022.out` in your cwd (you can `tail -f` it)
